notes: Canonical task manifest for the PM workbench. modality: text|image|audio|video. input: path relative to project root (text inputs are inlined; media are passed natively / as frames). hero=true marks the 7 prompts also run head-to-head against Claude Opus 4.8. ALL inputs are synthetic/self-generated — safe to publish.

tasks.title: Stakeholder update email
tasks.prompt:
You are a senior product manager. Write a concise weekly stakeholder update email (subject + body, under 200 words, scannable) from these raw notes:
- shipped CSV export (beta) to 12 design partners
- onboarding flow A/B test: variant B +9% activation, rolling to 100%
- API rate-limit incident Tue, 40 min, root-caused, fix shipped
- hiring: backend role still open, 2 onsites this week
- risk: data-migration for EU tenants slipping ~1 week
Use a confident but honest tone. End with one clear ask.

tasks.title: Messy notes -> action items table
tasks.prompt: Turn the messy meeting notes below into a clean Markdown action-items table with columns: Action | Owner | Due | Priority. Infer owners/priority where reasonable and flag anything ambiguous in a short note under the table.

tasks.title: Rewrite blunt Slack msg for execs
tasks.prompt: Rewrite this blunt internal Slack message so it's appropriate for a message to the VP of Product — clear, calm, solution-oriented, no blame. Keep it short. Original: 'eng totally dropped the ball on the launch, the dashboard is broken AGAIN and nobody told sales. this is the third time. we look incompetent.'

tasks.title: RICE prioritization
tasks.prompt:
Act as a PM and prioritize these 5 features using RICE (Reach, Impact, Confidence, Effort). Make reasonable assumptions, show the RICE table with scores, and recommend the top 2 with one-line rationale.
Features: (1) SSO/SAML login, (2) bulk CSV import, (3) dark mode, (4) in-app notifications, (5) audit log export.

tasks.title: Goal -> plan -> JSON tasks -> risks
tasks.prompt:
Goal: launch a self-serve free trial for our B2B SaaS in 6 weeks.
Do THREE things in order, with clear headers:
1) A short phased plan (weeks 1-6).
2) A machine-readable JSON array of work items, each: {"id", "title", "owner_role", "week", "depends_on"}. Output valid JSON only in this section, inside a ```json fenced block.
3) A risk table (Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Mitigation).

tasks.title: Strict JSON extraction (schema)
tasks.prompt:
Extract structured data from the text and return ONLY valid JSON matching this schema (no prose): {"company": string, "contact": {"name": string, "email": string}, "plan": "free"|"pro"|"enterprise", "seats": number, "renewal_month": string}.
Text: 'Hi, this is Dana Okafor from Brightwave Health (dana.okafor@brightwave.example). We're on the Pro plan with 45 seats and our renewal is in November.'

tasks.title: Tool-routing decision (agentic)
tasks.prompt: You are an agent with these tools: search_docs(query), create_ticket(title, severity), send_email(to, subject, body). A user says: 'The billing page returns a 500 error for all enterprise customers since this morning — please get someone on it and let finance know.' Decide which tool calls to make and in what order. Output a JSON array of calls: [{"tool", "args"}]. Then one sentence explaining your reasoning.

tasks.title: Python: parse CSV + chart
tasks.prompt: Write a single, runnable Python script (stdlib + matplotlib only) that reads the CSV below, prints the top 3 products by total revenue, and saves a bar chart of revenue by product to 'revenue.png'. Include brief comments. The CSV is provided as INPUT.

tasks.title: SQL from schema
tasks.prompt: Given tables users(id, signup_date, plan) and events(user_id, name, ts), write a PostgreSQL query returning, for each plan, the 7-day activation rate: % of users who fired an event named 'activated' within 7 days of signup. Explain the query in 2 sentences.

tasks.title: Explain + refactor snippet
Regex + test cases
tasks.prompt: Write a regular expression that matches order IDs of the form 'ORD-' followed by exactly 8 digits, optionally suffixed with '-R' for returns (e.g., ORD-12345678 or ORD-12345678-R). Provide the regex, then 4 should-match and 4 should-not-match examples in a table.

tasks.title: Summarize long PRD (256K ctx)
tasks.prompt: You are a PM lead reviewing a long PRD. Produce: (1) a 4-sentence TL;DR, (2) Key Decisions as bullets, (3) Open Risks/Questions as bullets, (4) one 'what's missing' observation. Be specific and cite section names where possible.

tasks.title: Condense support thread
tasks.prompt: Summarize this customer support thread into: Current status (1 line), Root cause if known, Next step + owner, and Customer sentiment (positive/neutral/negative with a short reason).

tasks.title: Extract decisions from transcript
tasks.prompt: From this meeting transcript, extract: Decisions made (bullets), Action items (Owner -> task -> due if mentioned), and any Parking-lot/Deferred items. Ignore small talk.

tasks.title: Read dashboard screenshot -> KPIs
tasks.prompt: This is a screenshot of a product metrics dashboard. Read it and report: each KPI with its value and trend, the single most concerning number and why, and one recommended action. Be precise about the numbers you see.

tasks.title: Chart -> data table
tasks.prompt: Extract the data shown in this bar chart into a Markdown table (Category | Value). Then state which category is highest and the approximate total.

tasks.title: OCR a scanned memo
tasks.prompt: Transcribe all the text visible in this scanned memo image exactly, then give a one-line summary of what it asks the reader to do.

tasks.title: Critique a UI mockup
tasks.prompt: This is a mockup of a mobile app screen. Describe the screen, identify 3 specific usability or visual issues, and suggest a concrete fix for each.

tasks.title: Voice memo -> summary + actions
tasks.prompt: Transcribe this voice memo, then give a 2-sentence summary and a bulleted list of any action items with owners if mentioned.

tasks.title: 2-speaker clip -> diarized + decision
tasks.prompt: This clip has two speakers. Produce a diarized transcript labeling 'Speaker A' and 'Speaker B', then state the decision they reached in one sentence.

tasks.title: Summarize screen-demo video
tasks.prompt: These frames are sampled from a short product demo recording. Describe what is being shown step by step, then write a 2-sentence summary a PM could paste into release notes.

tasks.title: List features shown in video
tasks.prompt: From these video frames, list every distinct feature name or screen title you can read on screen, in the order they appear.
